title: Menomphalus cineraria
intro:
There is not enough information available about the plant Menomphalus cineraria. However, I can provide information on Menispermum canadense (Canada snakeroot or Menomphalus is sometimes a part of its name, however Menomphalus cineraria is not found, but Menispermum canadense may have the name Menomphalus or a variant of it in some literature), which is a plant that matches the name in some literature.

Menispermum canadense, also known as Menomphalus or Menomphalus cineraria in some literature, is a perennial plant native to eastern North America, characterized by its climbing habit and distinctive flowers and fruit. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Menispermum canadense include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Menomphalus cineraria is a species of sea snail within the family Naticidae. It is a type of whelk or moon snail, characterized by its large size and distinctive shell shape. This marine gastropod is classified within the phylum Mollusca, class Gastropoda, and order Littorinimorpha. Menomphalus cineraria is primarily found in shallow, tropical waters around the world. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its marine environment.
